title: Advanced Simulation Software Developer
description:
**Requisition ID:** 24882
  
**Job Category:** Engineering & Technology
  
**Career level:** Professional
  
**Contract type:** Permanent
  
  

**Location:**  

Gothenburg, SE
  
  

**#job-location.job-location-inline { display:** inline; }
  
  

Do you want to be part of an ambitious and collaborative team, building world-class simulation technology used in real industrial applications?
  
  

Then you may be our next...
  
  

Advanced Simulation Software Developer
  
  

Digital Products Engineering is responsible for SKF’s portfolio of engineering and simulation software. We work closely with product development, research, application engineering, and customers worldwide to push the boundaries of bearing simulation.
  
  

In our Gothenburg-based team, you will contribute to the development of Beast (BEAring Simulation Tool) — SKF’s most advanced simulation platform, used for analysis and prediction of bearing application performance.
  
  

Key responsibilities
  
  

Drive the development and verification of new simulation technology
  
Work within domains such as multibody dynamics, contact mechanics, tribology, finite element methods, co-simulation, high-performance computing, high-fidelity 3D and surface geometry modelling, and advanced postprocessing
  
Collaborate with users and stakeholders to translate needs into technical improvements and effective UI and model building tools
  
Engage with product development and application engineering to ensure we deliver measurable impact on product performance, engineering efficiency, and business decisions
  
Contribute to the exploration and integration of AI to enhance simulation software capabilities and engineering efficiency
  
Contribute to the definition and evolution of simple, efficient, and modern design principles
  
Provide advanced technical support in complex customer cases
  
  

Requirements
  
  

PhD in mechanical engineering, engineering physics, computer science, mathematics, or similar, or MSc with relevant industry experience
  
Experience in developing simulation technology, engineering software, or scientific computing techniques
  
Proficiency in programming languages like C++, paired with sound judgment for writing clear, maintainable, and efficient code
  
Experience with high-performance computing, contact mechanics and tribology, advanced usage or development of commercial engineering software, or application of AI to support engineering software, are especially meritorious
  
Good communication skills and professional fluency in English
  
  

**You will enjoy working here if you:**  

are motivated by developing advanced simulation technology that helps engineers solve complex, real-world engineering problems
  
enjoy working at the intersection of physics and software development
  
thrive in a collaborative, engaged, knowledge-sharing environment
  
value simplicity, clarity, and high engineering standards
  
are motivated by seeing your work create real impact across engineering and product development
  
  

Our offer
  
  

The chosen candidate will work within an international team developing one of the world’s leading bearing simulation platforms, used across SKF in product development and engineering.
  
This role offers a unique combination of advanced physics, high‑performance computing, and real industrial impact, where your work directly influences engineering decisions and product performance.
  
We offer competitive employment conditions and support your professional development within SKF.
  
  

What you’ll love about SKF…
  
  

A culture of diversity and inclusion!
  
SKF is committed to creating a diverse environment, and we firmly believe that a diverse workforce is essential for our continued success. Therefore, we only focus on your experience, skills, and potential. Come asyou are – just be yourself #weareSKF
  
  

A Fair and transparent compensation
  
  

We provide transparent salary information in line with local legislations to eligible candidates and base our decisions on objective and gender-neutral criteria, such as professional experience, technical skills, to ensure fairness and consistency throughout the recruitment process.
  
  

Some additional information
  
  

This position will be based in Gothenburg, Sweden.
  
  

You will report to the Manager Beast Development, who is located in Gothenburg, Sweden.
  
  

For questions regarding the recruitment process, please contact Katrin Edelmann, Talent Acquisition Partner Europe & Africa, who is located in Schweinfurt, Germany, by email katrin.edelmann@skf.com. Please note that we can't accept applications via email.
  
  

Is this you?
  
If the answer is yes, submit your application with your CV and cover letter in English no later than Aug-31-2026.
  
  

To support a transparent process, we may include assessments and before hiring we can verify the information in your application in compliance with country-specific laws and regulations.

title: IT Consultant, Developer
description:
Netlight is a network organized and technology/product independent which means you will be able to work in many areas and industries, always focusing on your development. Competence development is core and realized through a culture of knowledge sharing, feedback and mentorship. Engagements where IT is business critical and edge. Netlight strives to be a truly diverse, equitable and inclusive organization. Regardless of who you are, what personality, background, and experience you have, you enrich our culture through your values. Netlight’s purpose is to unleash full potential, realize great careers, and fulfill personal growth. To achieve this, we have a highly engaged network organization, where every employee gets a mentor, delivery coach and personal communities.
  
Netlight provides a full range of premium IT-consulting services at the forefront of the digital industry and stands out for its product- and technology-independent expertise in a wide range of industries and clients. We are a network organization of more than 2 000 role models making aspiring digital leaders successful in their business-critical IT projects. Netlight has been awarded several times as top employer, as well as for growth, continued profitability and engagement for diversity, equity and equality within the IT industry. Today we are growing all across Europe, currently with more than 10 active international offices.
  
Application Process
  
We work continuously with finding the best talents on the market and have a dedicated Talent Search team in each office. Your application will be reviewed and we will get in touch within 2 weeks if we find that your profile matches our requirements. Apply by e-mail and attach your CV and a cover letter. Before applying, carefully read through qualifications below.
  
Minimum Qualifications
  
* BSc Degree in Technology
* Location (one of the following): Helsinki, Stockholm, Oslo, Munich, Berlin, Hamburg, Frankfurt, Zurich, Copenhagen, Amsterdam
* Verbal and written fluency in English
* Project experience in one of the fields mentioned in the job description
* Passion and potential to learn and grow
* Preferred Qualifications
* MSc preferably in Technology
* Native/fluent speaker: Finnish (Helsinki), Swedish (Stockholm), Norwegian (Oslo), German
* (Munich/Berlin/Hamburg/Zurich), Danish (Copenhagen) since we are located in thoes coutries. To our clients its importat to know local language to be able to deliver value, since much work is about local content. To global clients local language is not that important.
* Proven Track-record from previous positions (e.g. recognized achievements & deliveries)


Industry
  
Information Technology and Services, Computer Software, and Internet
  
Netlight Consulting is looking for professionals with high ambitions and the potential to become our next top consultants.
  
Job description
  
As a consultant at Netlight you will collaborate with top talented colleagues together with market leading clients. A Netlight consultant works with solving complex business critical challenges which demand excellence in innovative thinking and teamwork.
  
You will be working in an international networking organization and will have access to knowledge from Netlight’s consultants all across Europe. Projects range within competencies such as Frontend development, Backend development, Fullstack Development, Architecture, Business Intelligence, Data Engineering, Data Science, Data Analytics, Machine Learning, Cybersecurity, DevOps, Infrastructure, Test Automation, Agile coaching, Project Management, Product Ownership/Management, UX-/UI-/Interaction design and IT Strategy.

title: Game developer
description:
We are now looking for the best game developer to join us! You have the chance to be a part of creating the best casino games in the online slot industry!
   

  

**Job Description**
* Design, develop and maintain Casino Games for both desktop and mobile browsers
* The technologies you primarily will work with are HTML5, WebGL, Typescript, Pixi, GSAP and Javascript.
* In a team environment that consists of product management, graphic artistry, sound creation, server development and game mechanics, you will contribute to deliver the best casino games in the market today.
* Your main responsibilities are to make the game graphics come alive with spectacular animations, game flow, game mechanics and sounds according to a result delivered by our game servers.


 Who you are
   

  

* You are a Master Google Searcher that have Stackoverflow as your absolute favourite web site.
* You have practical experience of working with OOP in some language and you have a pragmatic approach to software development.
* Taking on technical challenges and solving them is something you take pride in.
* Quality, not only of the code you write, but the delivered system is important to you.
* You thrive in an environment where you are responsible for your code and take ownership of changes from editor to production system.


 Beneficial skills
   

  

* Working experience of mobile browsers and knowledge about their limitations and how they work in different ways is a big plus.


 Who we are
   

  

 We are the best and fastest growing slot provider in the world with the operator and product in focus, and the mobile future on our minds. Nolimit is a team of experienced professionals from the gambling industry in Stockholm, Sweden, Malta and Gurugram, India. Our company has derived from hands on work at some of the most successful gambling software providers and operators over the last 15 years. Our mission is to offer a fast, reliable and flexible team that cooperates with the best operators in the world, taking gambling to the next level.
   

  

 We have a clean code high performance modern mini/micro service portable system platform for mobile and desktop that is 100 % under our own control. Since we have our own platform, this allows us to deliver special features and smooth, customizable marketing campaigns that are tailored to the operator's specific needs. We think it's time for a new angle to the gambling software industry – one where the product is in focus and we fully engage their promotion, marketing and country manager system/activities with our games. Nolimit work with customers on a global scale and the largest gambling operators in the world have our games everywhere.
   

  

 Nolimit City, Stockholm based development team. We provide you with your choice of OS and IDE.
   

  

 Send your application to hr-sweden@nolimitcity.com
   

  

 http://www.nolimitcity.com

title: Fullstack Engineer
description:
We’re hiring engineers. Not ticket-takers.
   

  

 At Anyfin, we’re building financial infrastructure and products used by millions of people across Europe with a deliberately small engineering organisation.
   

  

 That means we care a lot about who we hire.
   

  

**What The Role Actually Looks Like**
 This is not a place where engineering sits separately from the business.
   

  

 Small cross-functional teams own their work end-to-end, from identifying problems and shaping solutions to shipping and operating them in production.
   

  

 Engineers here work closely with product, data and commercial teams to solve real-world problems across:
   

  

* Payments
* Credit decisioning
* Customer experience
* Operational tooling
* Internal AI systems
* Scaling infrastructure across markets


 There’s very little ticket-pushing and very few handoffs.
   

  

 We value people who can think independently, make sound tradeoffs and move things forward without unnecessary complexity.
   

  

**Tech**
* TypeScript
* React / React Native
* Node.js
* Python
* Go
* GraphQL
* PostgreSQL
* BigQuery
* Kubernetes
* GCP


 We also use AI heavily in day-to-day engineering work and product development.
   

  

 AI and machine learning have been part of Anyfin since the beginning, originally through credit assessment models, and increasingly across operations, tooling and product experiences.
   

  

 Today we work with multiple frontier models, build internal AI tooling and agent infrastructure, and are investing heavily in AI-powered financial experiences.
   

  

**Why engineers join**
**Because They Want**
* Real ownership
* Smart colleagues
* Modern tooling
* Complex systems without legacy bank infrastructure
* Fast decision-making
* High standards without unnecessary bureaucracy


 We’re intentionally not building a huge engineering organisation.
   

  

 We’d rather have fewer strong engineers with high trust, high expectations and meaningful impact.
   

  

**About Anyfin**
 Anyfin is a European fintech company helping people improve their financial well-being.
   

  

 We currently operate across multiple European markets and continue to expand rapidly, with ambitions far beyond refinancing alone.
   

  

 Our banking-light license opens the door to a much broader financial platform over time and we’re still early in that journey.
   

  

**A few practical notes**
* Stockholm based.
* EU work authorization required.


 Anyfin is a fintech on a mission to simplify loan repayments and improve the financial well-being of millions. We help consumers reduce the costs of high-interest loans, and take control of their repayment plan.

title: Software Engineers
description:
*MSAB is a global leader in digital forensic technology for mobile device examination and analysis. The MSAB vision is to empower every investigation with digital forensic solutions – for a safer world. A core value is to run an ethics-driven company with innovation and results. The sole focus of MSAB is on delivering high quality digital forensic solutions to collect, review, analyze and manage data. MSAB is a trusted partner to Law Enforcement, Defence Forces, Government Agencies and Forensic Laboratories in democratic countries throughout the world enabling them to investigate crime, gather intelligence, investigate fraud and fight corruption.* 




  





 To our new Stockholm office we’re looking for two engineers to join our Unicore team. You enjoy building systems that are not only functional, but also efficient, scalable, and thoughtfully designed. This is a chance to work with technology with a purpose – to create a safer world.
 



  





**The job** 




 At MSAB, the Unicore team is responsible for the core of our digital forensics platform, including UNIFY and the XRY file format (Core API). We’re now working on the next generation of UNIFY, and this is an opportunity to help shape it from the inside.
 



  





 In this role, you will work with core system development where performance and reliability are critical. You’ll be dealing with distributed systems and data-intensive workloads, contributing to architectural decisions with long-term impact, and exploring how to best leverage hardware such as CPU and GPU.
 



  





 Our current tech stack includes C99, C++20, and C# (.NET 8/10), along with gRPC, SQLite and PostgreSQL, running across both Windows and Linux environments.
 



  





**Who You Are** 




 We believe you will thrive in this environment if you have a genuine interest in programming and problem-solving, and if you care about performance, clean design, and user experience. You likely enjoy systems engineering, think in terms of trade-offs, and prefer solving meaningful problems over fitting into rigid roles. An appreciation for simplicity and keeping dependencies lean will serve you well.
 



  





**Your Experience** 




 You will benefit from your experience with DevOps and CI/CD practices, containerization with Docker, or cloud platforms such as AWS or Azure which is considered a plus.
 



 You will be part of a focused team working close to the core of the product, where your decisions matter and your code has real impact.
 



  





 Interested, or know someone who might be? Reach out or apply — we’d love to connect.
 



  





 There is much more to talk about, like values, our growth plans, technology choices etc., but we save that for the interview.
 



  





 Welcome to apply now!
 



  





 For more information about MSAB, please visit: http://www.msab.com
 



  





**What we can offer** 




 Create a safer world with us!
 



 As one of the world’s leading digital forensics companies, MSAB leads the way in pioneering advancements that contribute to making our world safer.
 



 Your work at MSAB will bridge the gap between technology and justice. You will be inspired to go beyond your comfort zone, and you will deal with challenging and meaningful projects. When you join us, you become part of a global team that aspires to create a safer digital future, one breakthrough at a time.
 



 We can offer you an exciting work environment, where we value being innovative, acting ethically and delivering results in everything we do while our customers' needs are at the center. We are proud to be a fast-growing international company with more than 20+ nationalities and we work with things that really make a difference.
 



 Every day at MSAB is an opportunity to redefine the future of digital forensics.
 



 Are you up for the challenge?
 



 Department Development Role C++ Developer Locations Stockholm, Sweden Remote status Hybrid

title: Full Stack Developer - Pacom
description:
**Securitas Group** 




  





 Securitas is a world-leading safety and security solutions partner that helps make your world a safer place. By leveraging technology in partnership with our clients, we offer a broad portfolio of value-enhancing services and solutions integrated across the security value chain – from on-site services to advanced monitoring, comprehensive risk prediction and advisory services.
 



  





 With around 322 000 employees in 44 markets, our innovative, holistic approach with local and global expertise makes us a trusted business partner to many of the world’s best-known companies. Benefitting from almost nine decades of deep experience and guided by our values of integrity, vigilance, and helpfulness, we create sustainable value by helping our clients optimize their operations and protect what matters most - their people and assets.
 



  





 Do you want to help develop cutting-edge security solutions?
 **Pacom** 
 is looking for a driven and engaged
 **Full Stack Developer with experience in C# .NET** 
 to strengthen our development team in Stockholm.
 



  





**Join Pacom – A Leading Provider of Integrated Security Solutions** 




  





**Pacom** 
 is an innovative company specializing in integrated security solutions and centrally managed security management for businesses and organizations with high security requirements. As part of the Securitas Group, we combine more than 40 years of innovation with a clear vision: to protect critical assets and simplify security operations for customers worldwide through advanced and scalable security platforms that consolidate functions such as:
 



  





 • Access Control
 



 • Video Surveillance
 



 • Fire and Intrusion Alarms
 



  





 Our solutions create a unified operator environment for smooth and efficient security management. We primarily serve businesses and government agencies where security is mission-critical. Learn more about us at
 
 pacom.com
 
 or
 
 pacomgroup.com
 
 .
 



  





  





**Your Role at Pacom** 




  





 As a
 **Full Stack Developer** 
 at
 **Pacom** 
 , you will play a key role in the development of our security solutions. You will work in an innovative and agile team where your ideas and contributions make a real impact.
 



  





**Your responsibilities include:** 




  





 • Developing and maintaining frontend and backend functionality for our security platforms.
 



 • Designing and implementing new features and solutions.
 



 • Collaborating with Product Owners and UX Designers to create user-friendly and efficient systems.
 



 • Ensuring high quality through code reviews, testing, and documentation.
 



 • Actively participating in the entire development lifecycle – from requirements analysis to deployment.
 



 • Contributing to the team’s technical growth and knowledge sharing.
 



  





  





**What We Are Looking For** 




  





 To succeed in the role of Full Stack Developer, we believe you have:
 



  





**Qualifications:** 




  





 • A relevant academic degree in IT or equivalent professional experience.
 



 • At least 7 years of experience in full stack development.
 



 • Experience developing in C# .NET.
 



 • Experience with frontend development using JavaScript, HTML, and CSS, preferably with frameworks such as Blazor.
 



 • Good understanding of database solutions (e.g., SQL Server).
 



 • Experience working with APIs and integrations.
 



 • Knowledge of DevOps processes and tools is considered an advantage.
 



 • Fluency in both Swedish and English, written and spoken.
 



 • Swedish citizenship is required, as the position may involve assignment to a security-classified role. Employment is also subject to a successful security clearance process.
 



 • Ability to lead a small team or assigned project.
 



  





**Personal Attributes:** 




  





 • Strong problem-solving and analytical skills.
 



 • Excellent communication skills and a collaborative team-player mindset.
 



 • Structured and detail-oriented, with a strong focus on quality.
 



 • Curious, engaged, and eager to learn and develop new technologies.
 



  





  





**Your Future at Pacom** 




  





 At Pacom, you will have the opportunity to become part of a dynamic, sales-, technology-, and development-driven company in an exciting growth phase. We offer an environment where innovation and collaboration are at the core, and where you will have the opportunity to:
 



  





 • Participate in the development of market-leading security solutions.
 



 • Be part of our journey toward the future of security technology.
 



 • Work on socially important projects that contribute to safety and security.
 



 • Join a skilled and committed team that supports and inspires one another.
 



 • Work in modern offices in Solna, Stockholm, with excellent transport connections.
 



 • Enjoy competitive employment terms, including opportunities for personal and professional development.
 



  





**Working conditions** 




  




* This role is open for candidates based in
 **Solna, Sweden.**
* Swedish citizenship is required, as the position may involve assignment to a security-classified role. Employment is also subject to a successful security clearance process.



  





**Application** 




  





 We are looking for someone who is engaged, curious, and ready to contribute to our success.
 



  





 Would you like to be part of our team and help create the next generation of security solutions? Welcome to Pacom – where innovation meets security.
 



  





 If you have any questions about the role, please do not hesitate to contact us. We look forward to receiving your application

title: Fullstack-utvecklare
description:
Till HiQ söker vi nu erfarna Fullstack-utvecklare som vill vara med och skapa innovativa och användarvänliga lösningar för våra kunder. I rollen kommer du fokusera på backend-utveckling och bidra till projekt som gör skillnad för samhället. Du blir en viktig del av ett kompetent team där ni tillsammans hanterar utvecklingen av framtidens digitala tjänster.
   

  

**Om rollen:**
 Som Fullstack-utvecklare kommer du främst att arbeta med backend-utveckling, men även ha möjlighet att bidra med frontend-kompetens när det behövs. Vi söker dig som har erfarenhet av:
   

  

* Backend: Java (Spring, Spring Boot) eller .NET/C#
* Frontend: Javascript/Typescript (React.js, Vue.js eller Angular)
* Molnlösningar: AWS, GCP eller Azure


 Du kommer att delta i hela utvecklingsprocessen från design och utveckling till test och driftsättning, samt bidra med tekniskt innovativa lösningar.
   

  

**Vi söker dig som har:**
* Minst 5 års erfarenhet av fullstack-utveckling
* Ingenjörsexamen i datateknik, teknisk fysik, elektroteknik eller motsvarande
* Svenskt medborgarskap och är flytande i svenska och engelska


**Meriterande:**
* Erfarenhet av DevOps: Docker, Kubernetes och CI/CD-pipelines
* Erfarenhet av molnplattformar (AWS, Azure, GCP)


**Vi erbjuder dig**
* Utmanande uppdrag både ute i våra kunders organisationer samt i vår egen regi, där HiQ har helhetsansvar för projekten.
* Möjlighet att delta i och leda utvecklingen av våra kunders framtida tjänster som förenklar människors vardag
* Kunniga kollegor som gärna delar med sig av sina kunskaper och erfarenheter
* En kreativ arbetsmiljö med möjlighet att utvecklas som människa, kollega och bli bättre på det du redan är bäst på!
* Du blir en del av ett värderingsstyrt bolag som värdesätter glädje och gemenskap och hittar på mycket roligt tillsammans.


**Varför HiQ**
 Sedan 1995 har HiQ varit konsultbolaget som förenklat världen för både kunder och slutanvändare med hjälp av teknik, design och kommunikation. HiQ har alltid fokuserat på två saker: att ha kul på jobbet och att leverera lösningar utöver förväntningar - det är vad som driver oss framåt! Vi HiQare är stolta över att ha en kultur som präglas av
 **resultat** 
 ,
 **ansvar, enkelhet** 
 och
 **glädje** 
 . Vi tror på att arbeta tillsammans för att nå våra gemensamma mål och strävar efter att hjälpa varandra att fortsätta växa och utvecklas. Hos oss får du utöver chansen att utveckla din egen kompetens också möjlighet att lära, leda och inspirera dina kollegor.
   

  

*I denna rekrytering tillämpar vi löpande urval och tjänsten kan komma att tillsättas innan ansökningstiden har gått ut. För att göra rekryteringsprocessen så rättvis och objektiv som möjligt kommer det att ingå urvalsfrågor, intervjuer, tekniska tester, referenstagning och bakgrundskontroll. Varmt välkommen med din ansökan!*
*Just nu tankar vi energi på semestern och är därför lite långsammare på bollen än vanligt. Vi är tillbaka i början av augusti och hör av oss då – tills dess hoppas vi att du njuter av en riktigt skön sommar! 🌞*

title: Data Engineer
description:
Data EngineerBuild the future of data, not just the pipelines.
   

 Most data engineering roles are centred around moving data from A to B.
   

 This isn't one of them.
   

 We're working with one of the Nordics' most exciting technology businesses as they continue to invest heavily in their data platform, AI capabilities and engineering function. They're looking for a Data Engineer who enjoys building complete solutions - someone equally comfortable modelling data, building APIs, automating workflows and solving business problems with technology.
   

 The data is already flowing. The challenge now is deciding what to build with it.
   

 You'll join a small, highly collaborative team where you'll have genuine ownership over the direction of the platform. Rather than sitting behind tickets all day, you'll work closely with stakeholders across the business to understand problems, design scalable solutions and build the tools that allow the company to make smarter decisions every day.
   

 If you've ever found yourself wanting to build more than pipelines, this is the role you've been looking for.
   

 The Opportunity:The business has spent the last few years modernising its data platform, creating a cloud-native data warehouse and establishing strong engineering foundations.
   

 Now comes the exciting part.
   

 The focus has shifted from simply collecting data to building intelligent services around it.
   

 You'll help design scalable data models that become the foundation for reporting, analytics and AI initiatives. You'll build lightweight applications that allow business users to interact with data. You'll expose datasets through APIs, automate repetitive processes and help create the infrastructure that powers a growing number of AI-driven workflows.
   

 Because the team is small, you'll see the direct impact of everything you build.
   

 Ideas don't spend months in approval chains. Engineers are trusted to identify problems, propose solutions and own them from design through to production.
   

 What you'll be working on:Your work will span far more than traditional data engineering.
   

 You'll be involved in:Designing scalable, well-structured data models using SQL and dbtBuilding Python services that automate business processesCreating APIs that expose trusted data to internal applications and AI servicesDeveloping custom integrations where off-the-shelf tooling isn't enoughBuilding cloud infrastructure using Infrastructure as CodeImproving data quality, testing and platform reliabilityWorking closely with commercial teams to understand how data can solve real business challengesHelping shape the next generation of AI-enabled products and internal tooling
   

 The engineering philosophy is simple:
   

 Solve business problems with technology.
   

 Sometimes that's a data model.Sometimes it's an API.Sometimes it's a small application.
   

 Whatever creates the most value.
   

 What they're looking for:This role would suit someone who's naturally curious and enjoys solving problems.You're probably someone who starts with "What are we trying to achieve?" rather than "Which technology are we using?"
   

 You'll bring:Strong Data Engineering FundamentalsExcellent SQL skillsStrong experience with dbtSolid Python development experienceExperience working with cloud data platformsAn understanding of modern data architecture and best practices
   

 Broader Engineering ExperienceThe ideal person isn't purely a pipeline engineer.You've probably built some combination of:REST APIsInternal toolingAutomation servicesBackend applicationsInfrastructure with Terraform or similarData integrations
   

 You don't need to be an expert software engineer.You simply enjoy building whatever is needed to solve the problem.
   

 Mindset matters more than tools!The hiring manager is far more interested in engineers who can learn than people who've memorised a specific technology stack.
   

 Whether you've worked with Google Cloud, Azure or AWS isn't particularly important. What matters is that you've worked in environments where you've had to think for yourself, adapt quickly and wear multiple hats. Experience in start-ups, scale-ups or fast-moving product businesses will be highly valued.
   

 Likewise, engineers who understand both the technical and commercial side of data tend to thrive here.
   

 If you've spent time working closely with stakeholders, translating business questions into technical solutions and making data genuinely useful, you'll fit right in.
   

 Tech Stack:Current technologies include:PythonSQLdbtBigQueryGoogle Cloud PlatformDockerTerraformAirbyteExperience with every tool isn't expected.Strong engineering principles and a willingness to learn are far more important than matching every technology.
   

 Why this role?This is an opportunity to join a company at exactly the right stage.
   

 The foundations have been built.
   

 Now the team is focused on creating a truly modern data platform - one that enables self-service analytics, intelligent automation and AI-driven products across the business
   

 You'll join a highly visible team where your work is used daily by stakeholders throughout the organisation.
   

 There are no endless approval processes.No rigid role boundaries.No spending months maintaining legacy pipelines.
   

 Instead you'll work alongside ambitious engineers and business stakeholders who genuinely care about building great products and using data to drive better decisions.
   

 It's the kind of environment where people who enjoy ownership, autonomy and variety do their best work.
   

 Salary & Benefits55,000–70,000 SEK per month (Dependent on experience)Hybrid working (Tuesday–Thursday in the Stockholm office)Comprehensive benefits packagePermanent position
   

 Interview ProcessInitial conversation with the Hiring ManagerTechnical reasoning and problem-solving case study (discussion-based, no live coding)Final technical and cultural interview

title: Software Engineer, Platform (Runtime)
description:
**About Lovable & The Apps Platform**
 Lovable is an AI-powered software creation platform. Millions of users use Lovable to go from idea to working application, generating hundreds of thousands of apps every day.
   

  

 The Lovable Apps Platform is what makes those generated apps actually run in production. The moment a user hits deploy, the Apps Platform takes over — provisioning the database, handling user auth, storing files, serving the app over a custom domain, capturing logs, and metering usage. It's the full backend and hosting layer for every app built on Lovable, designed so creators never have to think about infrastructure to ship something real.
   

  

 The Apps Platform is growing fast, and we're investing heavily in each layer of the stack — building deeper ownership over the services that power our users' apps so we can move faster, scale better, and shape the product experience us and our users actually want.
   

  

**The Role**
 We’re looking for software engineers with a platform mindset to work on the app runtime, deployment pipeline, and the composable managed services we purchase and operate. Some foundational services we buy from 3p partners, others are not available for purchase and you would be the first in the world to build them at scale. You'll keep these services running reliably, manage vendor relationships and migrations, and ensure operational excellence across the stack.
   

  

**What You'll Do**
* Own the app hosting and deployment pipeline — the runtime that serves user applications in production
* Manage and operate composable managed services for storage, logging, workflows etc
* Build and maintain custom domains infrastructure, ensuring reliable SSL provisioning and DNS management at scale
* Drive vendor evaluation, onboarding, and migration as we continue decomposing our platform from a bundled BaaS to best-of-breed individual services
* Establish operational excellence practices: monitoring, alerting, incident response, runbooks, and capacity planning across all managed services


**What We're Looking For**
* Experience building and operating distributed systems
* Ideally 6+ years of experience in running and developing
* Strong background in platform engineering, SRE, or infrastructure operations
* Deep understanding of multitenant app hosting infrastructure, container orchestration, or edge runtimes
* Vendor management experience: evaluating services, negotiating contracts, planning migrations, and holding vendors accountable for SLAs
* Operational mindset: you think in uptime, latency percentiles, cost-per-request, and blast radius


**Nice to Have**
* Experience building and operating massively multitenant control planes
* Experience operating Cloudflare Workers, Pages, R2, or D1 at scale
* Hands on experience contribution to cloud infrastructure or CNCF projects (kubernetes, worked, envoy)
* Familiarity with ClickHouse or other columnar databases for log analytics
* Experience with managed database services (such as Supabase, Neon, AWS Aurora, Google Cloud SQL etc)
* Cost optimization experience — you've driven meaningful infrastructure cost reduction


**Our tech stack**
 We're building with tools that both humans and AI love. Lovable software engineers are capable of working across the whole stack. Examples of tech in our stack include:
   

  

* Frontend: React and Typescript.
* Backend: Golang and Rust.
* Cloud: Cloudflare, GCP, AWS.
* Data: Clickhouse, Firestore, Spanner, BigQuery.
* DevOps & Tooling: CI/CD pipelines, OTEL, Kubernetes, Terraform.


 And always on the lookout for what's next!
   

  

**About Your Application**
* Please submit your application in English - it’s our company language so you’ll be speaking lots of it if you join
* We treat all candidates equally - if you’re interested please apply through our careers portal

title: Frontend Engineer - Music
description:
The Rights Systems team builds and operates Spotify Rights Center (SRC), Spotify’s rights management platform that enables the company and its partners to identify, manage, and enforce music licensing rights across creator- and artist-generated content.
   

  

 Comparable in scope to YouTube’s Content ID, SRC is designed specifically for Spotify’s ecosystem and plays an important role in supporting new formats like video and other emerging content types. Over the past year the team launched SRC from inception to a fully operational production platform, now live and used by real rightsholders.
   

  

 The platform includes automated content scanning, policy management, enforcement pipelines, dispute workflows, and analytics. The team has grown from a single squad to three squads (approximately 29 engineers, designers, and product specialists) and continues to scale as a strategic investment area for Spotify over the next several years.
   

  

 What You'll Do
   

  

* Build and evolve the Spotify Rights Center web application used by music labels, publishers, and other rights holders to manage how their content appears on Spotify
* Collaborate closely with product managers, designers, and backend engineers to deliver intuitive and high-quality user experiences
* Develop complex workflows that support large datasets, territory-level permissions, and multi-step processes for managing rights and disputes
* Integrate frontend systems with multiple backend services to support scalable and reliable data flows
* Write performant, accessible, and well-tested frontend code using modern development practices
* Partner with backend engineers to design and evolve APIs that support the needs of the product
* Contribute to product discovery and ideation with engineers, researchers, and product specialists
* Help improve engineering practices and advocate for strong frontend architecture and quality standards
* Support a collaborative team environment that encourages learning, experimentation, and growth


 Who You Are
   

  

* You have experience building modern web applications using TypeScript and React or a similar framework
* You are comfortable managing complex application state and data flows
* You have experience integrating frontend systems with APIs such as REST, gRPC, or GraphQL
* You understand how to design responsive interfaces that handle loading states, pagination, streaming data, and error handling gracefully
* You care deeply about creating intuitive and accessible user experiences
* You have experience building enterprise or B2B applications with complex workflows, permissions, and large datasets
* You write high-quality code and validate it using modern testing approaches such as unit, integration, or end-to-end testing
* You take ownership of solutions from requirements through deployment and iteration based on user feedback
* You enjoy learning new domains and problem spaces and collaborating with teammates to solve meaningful problems


 Where You'll Be
   

  

* This role is based in London or Stockholm
* We offer you the flexibility to work where you work best! There will be some in person meetings, but still allows for flexibility to work from home


 Spotify is an equal opportunity employer. You are welcome at Spotify for who you are, no matter where you come from, what you look like, or what’s playing in your headphones. Our platform is for everyone, and so is our workplace. The more voices we have represented and amplified in our business, the more we will all thrive, contribute, and be forward-thinking! So bring us your personal experience, your perspectives, and your background. It’s in our differences that we will find the power to keep revolutionizing the way the world listens.
   

  

 At Spotify, we are passionate about inclusivity and making sure our entire recruitment process is accessible to everyone. We have ways to request reasonable accommodations during the interview process and help assist in what you need. If you need accommodations at any stage of the application or interview process, please let us know - we’re here to support you in any way we can.
   

  

 We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us. Find our AI notice here: https://lifeatspotify.com/ai-notice
   

  

 Today, we are the world’s most popular audio streaming subscription service.

title: Software Engineer | Data Solutions
description:
**About Brite**
 Founded in 2019, Brite Payments is a Swedish fintech and a leader in Instant Account-to-Account (A2A) payments. With connections to over 3,800+ banks, Brite enables consumers to pay directly from their bank account (also known as Pay by Bank) in real-time.
   

  

 With no signup or card details required, users authenticate using their bank’s usual ID method, which is not only convenient and secure but provides merchants with fast fund settlement, zero chargebacks, and reduced costs.
   

  

 Headquartered in Stockholm, Sweden, Brite operates across 27 European markets, utilising local payment rails, open banking, and our own proprietary infrastructure, Brite Instant Payments Network (IPN).
   

  

 Want to join us on our journey? The future is Brite!
   

  

****The mission****
 Join the Data Solutions team at Brite! We aren't just maintaining pipelines; we are architecting the future of our data ecosystem. Having launched just a year ago, we occupy the perfect sweet spot: we’ve moved past the initial "blank page" phase, yet we are entirely free from legacy debt. If you have a passion for building high-impact data products from the ground up using a modern, serverless stack on GCP, this is your stage.
   

  

****What we’re looking for****
 We need a builder who thrives in an iterative environment. You won't just be executing tickets; you’ll be shaping our technical DNA. We value:
   

  

* True Ownership: You don’t just write code; you and the team own the solution from ideation to deployment.
* Analytical Rigor: You enjoy deconstructing complex data problems to find elegant, scalable solutions.
* The Power of "We": You believe that while individual talent is great, the best architecture is born from collaboration and collective brainpower.


**Requirements**
* Experience: 5+ years of Software Engineering experience, specifically building and deploying cloud-native services.
* The Stack: Deep proficiency in Python and a "modern-first" approach to development.
* Autonomy: A proactive mindset and the ability to navigate a high degree of freedom with responsibility.
* Presence: A desire to collaborate in person with our team at our Stockholm office. We are working on-site at least 3 days per week.


****Meritorious****
* Experience with Google Cloud Platform (GCP) and its ecosystem.
* A background in serverless architectures and building for the cloud.


****Why join Brite?****
 If you’re looking for a role where your work has a direct line of sight to the company’s success, you’ve found it. We offer an environment defined by innovation, zero "red tape" legacy, and a culture of mutual respect.
   

  

 Join us and build the next generation of data solutions at Brite.

title: Artificial Intelligence Engineer
description:
Full Stack AI Engineer Stockholm | Permanent | Hybrid (Primarily Onsite)
   

 Build AI that actually gets used.
   

 We're partnering with an ambitious, AI-native technology consultancy that's redefining how organisations adopt artificial intelligence. Rather than building demos or proof-of-concepts that never leave the lab, this team develops production-ready AI applications that solve real business problems and become part of clients' day-to-day operations.
   

 Founded by recognised AI leaders with extensive experience delivering enterprise-scale AI solutions, the business has quickly established itself as one of the fastest-growing specialist consultancies in the Nordic market. Backed by strong demand and an impressive client pipeline, they're now looking for a Full Stack AI Engineer with a passion for front-end development to help shape the next phase of their growth.
   

 If you're excited by AI, enjoy working directly with clients, and want to build products that make a genuine impact, this is an opportunity to join early and grow alongside some of the region's most experienced AI practitioners.
   

 The Role:This is a full-stack engineering position with a strong emphasis on front-end development and user experience.
   

 You'll work closely with clients to understand business challenges before designing and building AI-powered applications that automate complex processes across areas such as customer service, finance, procurement and internal operations.
   

 Working as part of a collaborative engineering team, you'll build intuitive interfaces, develop scalable back-end services and integrate modern AI capabilities into production environments.You'll be involved throughout the full project lifecycle, from workshops and solution design through to implementation and deployment.
   

 What you'll be working with:ReactTypeScriptPythonMicrosoft AzureREST APIsPostgreSQLLarge Language Models (LLMs)Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)Modern agentic AI frameworks
   

 We're looking for someone who has:Strong commercial experience with React and TypeScript.A genuine passion for creating polished, intuitive user experiences.Full-stack development experience including Python, APIs and cloud technologies.An interest in AI engineering and modern Generative AI technologies.Excellent problem-solving skills and a curiosity for learning new technologies.Strong communication skills with the confidence to work directly with clients.Professional fluency in Swedish (or another Nordic language) alongside English.
   

 Commercial AI experience is welcomed but not essential. If you have strong software engineering fundamentals and are passionate about AI, we'd love to hear from you.
   

 Why Join?Learn from exceptional mentorsWork alongside senior AI specialists who have spent years delivering enterprise AI solutions at scale. You'll be supported by leaders who genuinely enjoy mentoring engineers and helping them develop both technically and professionally.
   

 Build AI that goes into productionYour work won't sit in a prototype environment. You'll develop applications that are deployed into real businesses, solving meaningful operational challenges for clients.
   

 Be part of an AI-first engineering cultureAI is embedded throughout the company's own development processes. Engineers actively use AI-assisted development, modern tooling and intelligent workflows to build software faster and smarter.
   

 High ownership from day oneYou'll have genuine influence over technical decisions, product direction and delivery, working in a collaborative environment where ideas are encouraged regardless of seniority.
   

 Work directly with clientsYou'll help shape solutions from the earliest conversations through to production, giving you exposure to both technical delivery and consulting.
   

 Grow with the businessJoining at this stage offers significant opportunities as the company continues to expand. As the team grows, so will opportunities to specialise, lead projects and help define future AI offerings.
   

 CultureThis is a people-first business that hires carefully, prioritising curiosity, ambition and collaboration over simply increasing headcount. The environment is highly supportive, low on ego and built around continuous learning, knowledge sharing and delivering exceptional engineering.The team values diversity of thought and believes the best ideas come from collaborative problem-solving.
   

 LocationThe team works primarily from its Stockholm office, believing that close collaboration creates better outcomes for both engineers and clients. You should already be based locally or be willing to relocate.
   

 Interested?Whether you're already building AI applications or you're an experienced software engineer looking to specialise in Generative AI, this is an opportunity to work on cutting-edge technology while learning from some of the most experienced AI professionals in the region.
   

 If you'd like to find out more, we'd love to arrange an informal conversation.

title: Software Engineer - Internal Tooling
description:
Back to jobs
   

  

 Software Engineer - Internal Tooling
   

  

 Tandem Health
   

  

 Engineering
   

  

 Full-time
   

  

 On-site
   

  

 Stockholm
   

  

 Posted on 5/4/2026
   

  

**Job Description**
 Build something monumental for Healthcare!
   

  

 At Tandem Health we’re reimagining healthcare by putting clinicians first. Our platform - designed by clinicians, for clinicians - is built on deep insight into real-world pain points, with intuitive medical notes and workflows that truly support patient care.
   

  

 We’re a fast-scaling health-tech company backed by top investors and expanding globally. We move fast, stay curious, and believe building something that matters starts with an extraordinary team. If you're passionate about impact and innovation, we'd love to meet you!
   

  

**About The Role**
 You care about making systems work where it really matters, behind the scenes, where complexity tends to hide. At your previous role, you were likely drawn to problems that others avoided: messy logic, unclear ownership, and workflows that didn’t quite hold together. You enjoy bringing structure to that - building systems that are not just functional, but reliable and easy to reason about.
   

  

 At Tandem, you’ll work at the intersection of product, operations, and business logic, owning and building the internal systems that power how we operate at scale. A key focus for this role is
 **billing and invoicing** 
 , where product usage, access, and real-world workflows need to translate into correct, auditable invoices.
   

  

 This is not just about payments, it’s about deeply understanding how our product is used in practice, how that maps to our business model, and building the systems that make it all work seamlessly. You’ll work closely with engineering, operations, and commercial teams to ensure that what we build internally is robust, scalable, and aligned with how Tandem grows.
   

  

 If you enjoy working close to the business, taking ownership of complex logic, and building systems that directly impact how a company runs - this is your kind of role.
   

  

 What You Will Do
   

  

* Own and build Tandem’s internal tooling and operational systems, with a primary focus on billing and invoicing.
* Design and implement systems that connect product usage and access control to invoicing logic (e.g. integrations with Stripe).
* Build workflows that ensure invoices are generated correctly based on real-world product behavior and customer setups.
* Develop and maintain integrations between internal systems, databases, and external platforms (e.g. billing, CRM, analytics).
* Work closely with operations and commercial teams to understand business needs and translate them into robust technical solutions.
* Improve and automate internal processes to reduce manual work and increase reliability.
* Build internal APIs and services that support operational workflows and scale with the company.
* Take end-to-end ownership, from problem definition and system design to implementation, monitoring, and iteration.


 What You Bring
   

  

* Experience as a software engineer working on backend systems or internal tooling.
* Strong ability to model and implement business logic in code, especially in complex or evolving domains.
* Experience working with APIs, integrations, and data flows between systems.
* Familiarity with cloud environments (e.g. AWS, Azure, or similar).
* A pragmatic mindset - you focus on building systems that actually work in practice.
* Interest in working close to the business and understanding how technical systems map to real-world operations.
* Strong ownership and the ability to drive work independently in a fast-moving environment.


 Bonus Points
   

  

* Experience working with billing, invoicing, or financial systems (e.g. Stripe or similar platforms).
* Experience building internal tools or backoffice systems.
* Familiarity with data modeling for usage-based systems.
* Experience applying automation or AI to operational workflows
* Experience in regulated environments (healthcare, fintech, etc.).


**Location**
 We believe the best ideas happen when we’re together. This role is based in our vibrant HQ in the Epicenter building in the heart of Stockholm where you will work primarily from our office to collaborate, connect, and build our culture.
   

  

**How To Apply**
 We adopt a continuous selection process, so please make sure to apply with your CV in English.
   

  

**Our Interview Process Consists Of 4 Stages**
* Screening interview with Talent Acquisition
* Technical interview with some of our Engineers
* Interview with our CTO & Co-Founder
* Home case and presentation at our office - Join us in the Stockholm office for a lunch, and a presentation to experience our our culture firsthand, collaborate with our team, and see how you work in action.


**Benefits**
* Competitive salary & company stock options
* 30 days/year of paid vacation
* Pension
* 5,000 SEK wellness allowance (friskvårdsbidrag)
* Social and team-building activities (off-sites, after works, winter/summer parties)
* An opportunity to make a real positive impact in the world of healthcare
* Work with some of the best minds in AI, healthcare, and engineering.


 Culture at Tandem
   

  

 At Tandem, we move fast, think big, and take ownership. We're a high-performing, diverse team with a shared drive to change the future of healthcare - and we’re just getting started.
   

  

 Our culture is built on action, ambition, and learning. You'll be trusted to take the lead, challenge yourself, and make an impact from day one. We believe real growth happens when you're stretched, supported, and surrounded by smart, passionate teammates who want to win together.
   

  

 Even though we’re spread across countries, we come together often in Sweden for team meetings, social events, and offsites - blending global reach with real human connection.
   

  

 We hire for talent, potential, and attitude - valuing different backgrounds and fresh perspectives. Great ideas come from everywhere, and we’re building a team that reflects the world we want to change.
   

  

*Tandem handles sensitive patient data and will conduct a background check before hiring any candidate.*
 Apply for this position

title: Produktutvecklare - App
description:
****Vill du bygga appar med användarupplevelsen i fokus?****
 Nu söker vi dig som vill vara med och bygga appar där upplevelsen är lika viktig som funktionaliteten. Det ska vara snabbt, intuitivt och kännas genomtänkt i varje detalj.
   

  

 Hos oss blir du en del av ett produktutvecklingsteam där du jobbar tätt tillsammans med andra utvecklare där ni gemensamt ansvarar för helheten i produkten.
   

  

 Compileit startades av Isak Utegård och Jonathan Ortheden - två utvecklare som ville skapa digitala produkter med slutanvändaren i fokus. Sedan starten har vi varit med och byggt några av världens mest nedladdade appar och fortsätter i dag att utveckla digitala produkter och AI-lösningar tillsammans med våra kunder. Genom vår expertis har vi hjälpt kunder som Hästens, SkiStar, ICA och Barncancerfonden att nå nya höjder.
   

  

 Vi jobbar nära våra kunder och är ofta med från tidiga idéer till färdig produkt. Det betyder att du får vara med och påverka både tekniskt och produktmässigt. Vi är inte låsta vid en specifik plattform eller teknik, utan istället väljer vi det som passar bäst för situationen.
   

  

****Du kommer bland annat att:****
* Omsätta idéer och design till genomarbetade appupplevelser med hög kvalitet i varje detalj
* Utveckla applikationer som är snabba, stabila och optimerade för verklig användning
* Arbeta aktivt med prestanda och hur appar beter sig i praktiken.
* Säkerställa en sömlös integration med backend och en robust helhet
* Bidra till tekniska beslut som formar både arkitektur och användarupplevelse


****Vem vi tror att du är****
 Nyfikenheten driver dig. Du gillar att lösa problem, särskilt de som kräver att man tänker ett varv extra. Du vill förstå varför något fungerar som det gör och brinner för att skapa enkla men kraftfulla produkter även när tekniken bakom är komplex.
   

  

 Du håller dig gärna uppdaterad på vad som händer på plattformarna – kanske spanar du in vad som presenteras på WWDC eller Google I/O.
   

  

 Och precis som många här på Compileit händer det att du fastnar i små hobbyprojekt – helt enkelt för att du gillar att utforska idéer och vill utveckla dem vidare. Vi söker dig som brinner för användarupplevelsen och de unika möjligheterna som finns hos mobila plattformar, oavsett om din bakgrund är inom cross-platform (Kotlin multiplatform, React-Native) eller native (SwiftUI, Jetpack Compose) mobilutveckling.
   

  

**Vi tror att du har:**
* 1-3 års erfarenhet av apputveckling
* Erfarenhet av att utveckla appar eller interaktiva produkter med höga krav på användarupplevelse
* God förståelse för hur appar fungerar i praktiken - från livscykel och state till prestanda och responsivitet
* Öga för detaljer och en vilja att skapa lösningar som känns genomarbetade, inte bara fungerar
* Bekväm med att röra dig mellan olika tekniker beroende på vad produkten kräver
* Erfarenhet av cross-platform-utveckling är meriterande
* Kunskap/intresse för apple/google design guidelines är meriterande
* Erfarenhet av att jobba nära design och omsätta UI/UX till färdiga upplevelser
* Kommunicera tydligt på svenska och engelska
* En prestigelös inställning till både teknik och kollegor


****Vad får du som anställd hos oss?****
 ☀️ 6 veckors semester
   

  

 ⛷️ Maxat friskvårdsbidrag
   

  

 👩🏽‍💻 Generösa arbetsverktyg
   

  

 🏢 Centralt kontor
   

  

 🚚 Flyttdag
   

  

 👵🏼 5 % tjänstepension
   

  

 🚀 Stort utrymme att påverka – du är en medskapare av vår framtid
   

  

 🫶 Ett team som gillar att utforska, dela kunskap och bygga saker som får andra att säga
 *”hur gjorde ni det där?”*
 ⏱️ Ingen tidsrapportering – vi bryr oss mer om vad du skapar än hur du loggar timmar
   

  

**Vi söker flera apputvecklare till både pågående och kommande projekt.
   

  

 Vi intervjuar löpande så hör av dig redan i dag.**
**Hoppas vi hörs och ses!**

title: Build the AI that runs the Nordics’ biggest entertainment group
description:
***We don’t need another slide about AI. We need someone to ship it.***
 Viaplay Group entertains millions every day – across streaming, sport, film, series, radio and TV. Behind that sits one of the deepest content libraries in the region: thousands of hours of sport and non-sport, millions of viewers, and a product touched by people every single evening.
   

  

 That scale is the opportunity. Every recommendation, every search, every onboarding flow, every workflow behind the scenes – all of it can be made smarter, faster and more personal with AI. We’re building an AI hub in Stockholm to make that happen, and we’re looking for the people who will actually build it.
   

  

**If you’ve been doing this in a startup – real AI work, limited budget, no safety net – imagine what you’d do with a giant audience, a mountain of content and a product that reaches the whole Nordics. This is that stage.**
**What You’ll Actually Do**
* Ship AI into the product customers touch – discovery, personalisation, search and the experiences that turn a browse into a play.
* Turn our content advantage into a product advantage: use sport and non-sport at scale to build things nobody else in the region can.
* Rip friction out of how we work – automate the repetitive, wire up agents and tools, and make teams dramatically faster.
* Prototype in days, not quarters. Prove it with real users. Then scale what works and kill what doesn’t.


**Who We’re Looking For**
 We care about what you’ve built, not the boxes you tick. You probably recognise yourself here:
   

  

* You build. You’ve shipped real AI – agents, models, tools – that solved an actual problem, not a demo.
* You move fast and think in outcomes. You’d rather have something live and learning than perfect on paper.
* You spot the opportunity in messy, complex systems and go straight for the highest-leverage fix.
* You challenge assumptions – including your own – and you don’t wait for permission to make things better.
* You bring people with you and create momentum when things are still undefined.
* You communicate clearly in English and can turn “technically possible” into something the whole business gets.
* Hands-on experience building AI agents for real business problems, or a background in software, product or digital transformation.


**How We Work**
 Substance over process. We’re a big company that wants to move like a small one – speed is our edge, and we protect it. You’ll have the reach of a big player in the markets and the freedom to actually make a difference. Show up, build, and let the work speak.
   

  

**What You Get**
* A rare canvas: massive audience, huge content library, and real ownership of what you ship.
* 30 days paid vacation, strong pension and insurance, and generous parental leave top-up.
* Wellbeing support including a wellness allowance, and a learning culture that puts you in the driver’s seat.
* Unlimited Viaplay – series, live events, new-release rentals, linear channels and more. Know the product you’re building for.


**Hit play**
 If this is your kind of challenge, apply with your CV (add your LinkedIn or GitHub while you’re at it). Applications by email can’t be accepted – but questions about the role are always welcome.
   

  

**Don’t send us a cover letter about AI. Tell us what you’ve built – and what you’d build here.**
 Want to learn more about who we are and what we do? Check out our careers page or follow us on Instagram! We’re only ever a few clicks away.

title: Verksamhetsutvecklare
description:
Är du redo för en större uppgift? Vi söker nu en verksamhetsutvecklare som kommer att arbeta med att kombinera framtidens polisiära utrustning med framtidens teknik. Polismyndigheten är Sveriges största myndighet med ca 40 000 medarbetare över hela land
   

  

**Organisation** 
 Nationella operativa avdelningen
 **Kommun** 
 Stockholm
 **Län** 
 Stockholms län
 **Utlyst** 
 5 juni 2026
 **Ansök senast** 
 5 augusti 2026
 **Referensnummer** 
 A365.966/2026
   

  

 Arbetsplatsbeskrivning
   

  

**Nationella operativa avdelningen (Noa)**
 Noa ansvarar för att stärka hela Polismyndigheten genom att driva utveckling och upprätthålla en strategisk nationell lägesbild över brottsligheten. Vidare samordnar Noa nationellt och internationellt arbete för brottsbekämpande verksamhet samt högspecialiserad och unik polisverksamhet som kompletterar polisregionerna vid behov. Noa eftersträvar flexibilitet där arbetsuppgifter och insatser styrs av prioriteringar. Anställda kan således komma att tas i anspråk för andra arbetsuppgifter när så krävs.
   

  

**Enheten för utveckling av brottsbekämpning (UB)**
 Enheten ansvarar för att samordna, inrikta och prioritera den strategiska förmågeutvecklingen och verkar för att Polismyndigheten över tid har en relevant brottsbekämpande förmåga. Detta genom att löpande analysera, förstå brottsutvecklingen och kontinuerligt anpassa Polisens förmåga. Utvecklingsarbetet sker utifrån operativa problem och i nära samarbete med övriga Polismyndigheten.
   

  

**Sektionen för polisiära metoder och utrustning**
 Sektionen består av fem grupper, varav två är specialiserade på polisiär utrustning. Nu söker vi dig som vill arbeta i gruppen
 *Teknik och Fordon,* 
 som har ansvar för polisutrustade fordon, trafikteknisk utrustning, sensorer, teknisk utrustning för specialfunktioner samt obemannade system, t.ex. UAS.
   

  

 Arbetsbeskrivning
   

  

*Teknik och Fordons* 
 uppdrag är att utforma tekniska kravspecifikationer för utrustning som ska användas i polisiär verksamhet. Vi arbetar inom områdena fordon, trafikteknisk utrustning (allt från fordonsmonterade radarsystem till spikmattor), UAS och andra obemannade system samt teknisk utrustning och hjälpmedel för såväl ordinarie yttre verksamhet som specialfunktioner (t.ex. spanings- och insatsgrupper). Vi har det nationella uppdraget att förse Polismyndighetens olika verksamhetsområden med rätt teknik. För att hålla oss uppdaterade på utvecklingen bevakar vi omvärlden genom att besöka mässor, leverantörer och andra myndigheter, både i Sverige och utomlands.
   

  

 Vi söker en person som har lätt för att anpassa sig efter nya uppdrag, ser synergieffekter mellan olika teknikområden och kan omsätta praktiska behov till kravspecifikationer. För att ställa rätt krav kommer du att samverka med de verksamheter som har behov av utrustningen samt med funktioner som har ett nationellt ansvar. Du behöver ha en mycket god förmåga att samarbeta med andra. Arbetet innebär både individuellt arbete och grupparbete. På sikt kommer du att ansvara för egna uppdrag och leda arbetsgrupper.
   

  

**Huvudsakliga arbetsuppgifter:**
* Framtagande av tekniska kravspecifikationer.
* Samverkan med andra enheter i upphandlingsarbetet.
* Genomförande av tester och utvärdering av utrustning.
* Omvärldsbevakning, t.ex. besök på mässor och hos leverantörer.
* Samverkan med myndigheter och organisationer, nationellt och internationellt.


 Kvalifikationer
   

  

 Detta är en möjlighet för dig som har:
   

  

* Högskoleutbildning eller annan utbildning som arbetsgivaren finner relevant, alternativt förvärvad erfarenhet som Polismyndigheten bedömer likvärdig.
* Erfarenhet och kunskap inom minst ett av våra arbetsområden: fordon eller UAS/obemannade system.
* God förmåga att uttrycka dig i tal och skrift på svenska och engelska.


 Det är meriterande om du även har:
   

  

* Ingenjörsexamen eller likvärdig utbildning/erfarenhet, gärna inom fordonsteknik, elektronik eller maskinteknik.
* Erfarenhet av att arbeta med flera av gruppens teknikområden (exempelvis både fordon och UAS).
* Erfarenhet av kravställning, upphandling eller liknande.
* Erfarenhet av projektarbete och utvecklingsarbete med inriktning på fordon eller teknik.
* Erfarenhet av arbete inom eller med större organisationer eller myndigheter.


**Personliga egenskaper**
 Tjänsten ställer krav på ett stort intresse för att identifiera morgondagens utvecklingsområden i syfte att ta fram kvalitativ utrustning. Arbetet är självständigt men du ingår i och bidrar till en grupp med många kontaktytor och deltar i samverkansgrupper. Du bör ha engagemang och förmåga att ta dig an utmaningar samt omsätta dessa till konkreta, mätbara resultat.
   

  

 Vi söker dig som:
   

  

* Trivs med spännvidd i arbetet: Du kan växla mellan olika prioriteringar och har förmåga och vilja att arbeta med allt från fordon till UAS och sensorer.
* Har god samarbetsförmåga och trivs med att bygga relationer över organisationsgränser.
* Är ansvarstagande och har en vilja att leda uppdrag och arbetsgrupper.


 Vi kommer att lägga stor vikt vid personliga egenskaper.
   

  

 Kontaktpersoner
   

  

 Har du frågor om uppdraget är du välkommen att kontakta rekryterande chef:
   

  

 Daniel Palfelt, 073-3315157.
   

  

 Har du frågor gällande rekryteringsprocessen kontakta ansvarig HR-konsult:
   

  

 Annica, 073-072 70 69.
   

  

 Fackliga företrädare
   

  

 Polisförbundet, Sofia Ask, tfn: 010-563 87 10
   

  

 SACO, nås via mejl saco-s.avdelningarna@polisen.se
   

  

 SEKO Polisen, Karna Tillheden, tfn: 010-561 66 27
   

  

 ST, st.inom-polisen@polisen.se, tfn: 010-563 32
   

  

 Övrig information
   

  

 Anställningsform: Tillsvidareanställning med provanställning om sex månader
   

  

 Arbetsort: Stockholm
   

  

 Arbetstid: Förtroendearbetstid
   

  

 Tillträde: Enligt överenskommelse
   

  

 Funktion: Verksamhetsutvecklare
   

  

 Ansök genom att bifoga ditt CV (vi önskar inte något personligt brev) och besvara urvalsfrågorna i ditt ansökningsmejl. Frågorna utgår från kravprofilen för den här rekryteringen och dina svar ligger till grund för det urval som sker i processen. Det är därför viktigt att du svarar tydligt och utförligt på frågorna och inte hänvisar till andra handlingar t.ex. CV.
   

  

**Att besvara frågorna är en förutsättning för att din ansökan ska anses komplett.**
**Besvara följande urvalsfrågor som en del av din ansökan:**
* Vilken utbildning har du? (Ange högskoleutbildning, ingenjörsexamen eller motsvarande erfarenhet som du anser relevant).

* Har du goda kunskaper i svenska och engelska i både tal och skrift?
* Har du körkort B? (Ja/nej)
* Har du svenskt medborgarskap? (Ja/nej)
* Har du erfarenhet av att utforma tekniska kravspecifikationer? Beskriv hur du har omsatt praktiska behov till krav inom teknik eller fordonsområdet.

* Har du erfarenhet av kravställning och upphandling? Beskriv din roll i dessa processer och gärna erfarenhet av samverkan med andra enheter i upphandlingsarbetet.
* Har du erfarenhet av arbete inom eller tillsammans med större organisationer eller myndigheter? Beskriv din erfarenhet av samverkan på nationell eller internationell nivå.
* Hur ser du på att arbeta självständigt med egna uppdrag samtidigt som du leder arbetsgrupper? Ge ett exempel på när du har visat ansvarstagande och lett ett arbete mot ett mätbart resultat.


 Vänligen notera att krav och eventuella meriter ska vara uppfyllda vid ansökningstillfället.
   

  

 De uppgifter du lämnar i samband med din ansökan kan användas för att kontakta dig, få information och kallelser samt eventuella anställningshandlingar.
   

  

 Arbetsprov och ev. tester kan komma att användas i rekryteringsprocessen.

 En anställning hos Polismyndigheten kan komma att inledas med en provanställning om sex månader.
